articles.title: REPORT]
articles.summary: How much does it cost to get a lead on Google Ads in 2026? This ungated report [4] from WordStream by LocaliQ lays out benchmarks for key metrics across 23 industries.

articles.title: TIKTOK WORLD 2026: IS THE MARKETING FUNNEL FINALLY DEAD?
articles.summary: TikTok is collapsing the traditional funnel as discovery, consideration, and purchase now happen in the same content experience. 64% of users buy after discovering a product on the platform, often without leaving it. TikTok Shop and creator-led formats embed commerce directly into content through episodic storytelling and in-feed purchase loops. Winning brands use native, culturally fluent videos that fit the feed rather than polished ads.

articles.title: REASONING LIFT: WHAT HAPPENS TO AI VISIBILITY WHEN AI THINKS HARDER
articles.summary: High reasoning mode changes AI visibility and should be treated like a separate search environment. GPT 5.2 increased citation rates from 50% to 68% and nearly doubled citations per response, with 4.6X more fan-out searches. Impact is strongest in mid and lower funnel comparison queries that trigger deep research across pricing, docs, compliance, APIs, and product details. Early funnel content also matters since early citations persist through buyer journeys. Brands need content that answers specific sub-queries and consistent visibility across reasoning modes.

articles.title: THE 1 SECOND THAT DECIDES IF YOUR VIDEO GOES VIRAL
articles.summary: Most captions are added as an afterthought and miss what holds attention. The first subtitle should appear at second 0 and stay under 6 words. Key phrases should be enlarged 1.5 to 2X for emphasis. Background contrast like a semi-transparent bar or drop shadow improves readability. Platform auto-captions should be turned off to avoid duplicate text. Subtitles should sit within the middle 60% of the frame to avoid TikTok, Instagram, and LinkedIn UI overlays.

articles.title: SWATCH SHOWS THAT CO-BRANDING IS MARKETING'S MOST UNDERRATED GROWTH LEVER
articles.summary: The Swatch x Audemars Piguet “Royal Pop” pocket watch triggered global queues, resale prices up to 15X retail, and emergency stock warnings. Demand for the $400 co-branded product was extreme. The collaboration shows co-branding as a strategic growth lever, not a tactical promotion. The pocket watch format avoids direct comparison with the $25K Royal Oak. It still borrows its distinctive design cues. AP gains exposure to new audiences, while Swatch gains credibility. Both brands increase demand and long-term brand value.

articles.title: LINKEDIN BEATS YOUTUBE FOR B2B VIDEO, PER WISTIA'S 2026 REPORT
articles.summary: Wistia's 6th annual 2026 State of Video Report [9] is here! Find out what 13 million video and 1,000 video marketers reveal about budgets, performance, and the impact of AI. Also - get benchmarks that can help you answer the age-old question: is this video actually working? Get

articles.title: MARKETING SKILLS FOR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: An open-source collection of markdown-based "skills" gives AI coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor structured marketing knowledge across 40+ tasks, from CRO and copywriting to SEO audits and pricing strategy. Each skill acts as a reusable prompt framework that agents reference automatically when they detect a relevant task.

articles.title: GOOGLE ADS BENCHMARKS 2026
articles.summary: Google Ads performance metrics showed unusual stability this year, with cost per lead declining for the first time in five years to an average of $66.69. Conversion rates improved across 87% of industries, suggesting advertisers are adapting to AI-driven features like Performance Max and AI Max. Meanwhile, CPC averaged $5.42, more than double the $2.32 recorded when the report launched in 2016.

articles.title: WE ALL NEED SOMEBODY TO LEAN ON: REMEDIES FOR FEELING ALONE ON A TEAM
articles.summary: Only 20% of the global workforce feels engaged at work, while 44% of US employees report burnout. Under-support is a structural issue that often hits during role changes, unclear leadership, or AI-driven workflow shifts, which forces employees to be their own coach or feedback loop. The remedy is to build lower friction support systems. This means setting clearer priorities, recurring peer check-ins, and more actionable manager communication.

articles.title: IC WORK IS THE NEW CAREER FLEX
articles.summary: AI is turning senior marketers and operators into high-impact individual contributors who can ship cross-functional work without large teams. Companies should reward business impact over headcount as one person can now do work that once required a PM, designer, engineer, and marketer. Faster execution changes decision-making by reducing the need for layers of approvals and coordination. Teams should hire people who can connect growth, product, and distribution end to end and give them direct access to business context and core metrics. At Lovable, 90% of time now goes to building instead of meetings and alignment.

articles.title: BFF ENERGY AT SCALE: TURNING CREATOR CONNECTION INTO REAL BUSINESS RESULTS
articles.summary: Creator ads on Snapchat drive stronger performance, including 66% higher excitement, 29% higher interest in other products, and 25% longer watch time than standard ads.

articles.title: PINTEREST WANTS BRANDS TO START THINKING ABOUT CHRISTMAS
articles.summary: Marketers should launch their expanded Q4 campaigns now to maximize their in-app opportunities.

articles.title: 23 TIPS TO BRAND BUILDING ON LINKEDIN
articles.summary: Tips include ungating your best info, commenting posts, and posting between 8-11 AM local time.
